title: Xanthisma wigginsii
intro: Xanthisma wigginsii, also known as Wiggins' desert marigold, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States. Research on Xanthisma wigginsii has revealed potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Xanthisma wigginsii is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Xanthisma wigginsii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to the southwestern United States. It belongs to the genus Xanthisma, which contains several species of plants commonly found in dry areas. Xanthisma wigginsii is a member of the tribe Millerieae and is closely related to other species within this group. The plant is also known by its common name, Wiggins' greenthread.
